title: limonium subrotundifolium
intro: Limonium subrotundifolium, also known as the small-leaved sea lavender, is a species of flowering plant in the family Plumbaginaceae. It has been used in traditional medicine for its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties, which have been attributed to the presence of flavonoids and phenolic acids. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, including reducing inflammation, fighting infections, and exhibiting antioxidant properties. Limonium subrotundifolium can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat a range of health issues, from skin conditions to respiratory problems.
